title: AI-DLC Enterprise Evaluation — Measuring AI-Driven Development Efficiency
abstract: A structured evaluation framework applying AI Development Lifecycle (AI-DLC) principles to assess enterprise readiness of AI coding tools — covering governance, security, compliance, cost management, and organizational change management across the tool evaluation pipeline.
why_we_care: Selecting AI coding tools based only on technical benchmarks ignores the enterprise realities of governance, security, and organizational adoption. This evaluation framework ensures our tool recommendations are defensible to leadership and account for total cost of ownership, compliance requirements, and the human side of tooling changes — especially critical as we prepare to recommend tools for broader team adoption.
summary: A structured framework for evaluating AI coding tools through enterprise lenses — governance, security, compliance, cost, and change management — using AI-DLC principles rather than just technical capability benchmarks.
description:
## Overview

This intell tracks our approach to evaluating AI coding tools through an enterprise lens using AI-DLC (AI Development Lifecycle) principles. Rather than evaluating tools purely on technical capability, this framework ensures we also assess:

## Evaluation Dimensions

- **Governance**: How does the tool fit into existing IT governance frameworks?
- **Security & compliance**: Data residency, SOC 2, code IP protection
- **Cost management**: Per-seat pricing vs. usage-based, TCO analysis
- **Change management**: Training needs, adoption curves, workflow disruption
- **Integration**: How does it fit with existing CI/CD, code review, and deployment pipelines?
- **Vendor risk**: Company stability, lock-in potential, exit strategy

## Application

Apply this framework to each coding tool intell (Copilot, Claude Code, Devin, Augment, Codex, etc.) to produce comparable enterprise-readiness scores.
